key_insight: A two-stage evolutionary hyperparameter search can make PINN training more robust by using cheap low-fidelity screening first and full training only for the most promising configurations.
engineering_relevance: This provides a practical way to improve PDE-solver accuracy under fixed compute budgets when PINNs are sensitive to hyperparameters and prone to unstable convergence.
method: Stage 1: run truncated, low-fidelity PINN trainings to evaluate many hyperparameter candidates quickly.
Use an evolutionary algorithm to search a mixed, noisy black-box hyperparameter space.
Stage 2: fully train only the top candidates with standard gradient-based optimizers.
evidence_signal: The approach was evaluated on Advection, Klein-Gordon, and Helmholtz problems and is reported to consistently beat standard training with significantly lower mean error under constrained computation.
watch_item: The low-fidelity screening may mis-rank candidates if early training behavior does not reflect final PINN performance, so budget allocation and selection criteria need care.
paper_title: Evolutionary Two-Stage Hyperparameter Optimization Strategies for Physics-Informed Neural Networks
